title: Inheritance 🧬
description: Don't write the same code twice! Let classes inherit traits from parents.

slides.title: The Family Tree 🌳
slides.content:
# Copying is Bad

Imagine you are building a Zoo app.
You have a `class Lion` (has name, age, eats, sleeps) and a `class Penguin` (has name, age, eats, sleeps).

Writing `name`, `age`, `eat()`, and `sleep()` inside EVERY single animal class is exhausting and violates the DRY rule (Don't Repeat Yourself).

Instead, we can create a Parent class and have the children **Inherit** from it!

slides.title: The `extends` Keyword 🧬

quizData.question: If `Lion extends Animal`, which statement is true?

options.text: The Lion loses all its own properties.
The Lion automatically gains all the properties and methods from the Animal class.
The Animal class gains the roar() method from the Lion.

quizData.explanation: Inheritance only flows downwards. The child (Lion) inherits everything from the parent (Animal), but the parent does not get the child's unique traits.

slides.title: Using Inherited Traits 🎁

quizData.explanation: Dart uses the 'extends' keyword to establish an inheritance relationship.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Both children get the same base properties, but act entirely differently!
